skill.name: blockrun
skill.description: Routes selected requests through the BlockRun SDK to paid external AI models for image generation, live X search, and second opinions.
skill.summary: Use BlockRun to call paid external AI models through an agent wallet when local Claude or Codex capabilities are not enough.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static backtick and weak-crypto detections are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and ordinary words in the documentation. However, the skill intentionally instructs agents to install and run a third-party SDK, create a hidden wallet session, spend funds, and send requests to external AI providers, so it requires human review before publication.

high_findings.title: Paid External Model Routing
high_findings.description: The skill directs agents to route user requests through BlockRun to OpenAI, xAI, Google, and other providers using x402 micropayments. This sends user content to external services and can spend wallet funds, so publication needs human review and clear user consent controls.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes wallet-funded routing to external providers and gives usage triggers. No malicious exfiltration is proven, but the paid network behavior is intentional and high impact.

high_findings.title: Third-Party SDK Installation and Execution
high_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to install or upgrade blockrun-llm with pip and then execute Python code from that package. This creates supply-chain and runtime risk because marketplace users must trust external package behavior outside the reviewed skill file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pip install and upgrade commands are explicit, and the skill repeatedly imports and runs the installed package. The risk is confirmed even though no malicious package behavior was found in this file.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Wallet Session Storage
medium_findings.description: The skill documents a wallet session stored under $HOME/.blockrun/.session. Hidden local wallet material increases impact if the SDK, host, or surrounding workflow mishandles file permissions or disclosure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden session path is explicitly documented. The exact contents and permissions are not shown, so the severity remains medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are caused by fenced Markdown examples and inline code markers, not Ruby shell execution. I found no Ruby code or shell backtick execution in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples or inline command text. There is no Ruby execution context in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections at the description and table header are keyword matches, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA-1, or other weak algorithm use in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML description text and line 180 is a Markdown table header. Neither location contains cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Initiated Paid Network Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill teaches the agent to make external model calls that can cost money and transmit user prompts to third-party providers. This pattern should require explicit user consent, budget limits, and disclosure.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill includes budget control guidance and concrete calls for live search and image generation. The pattern is intentional and user-visible, but it still carries financial and data-sharing risk.

content.user_title: Route Tasks to Paid AI Models
content.value_statement: Some tasks need image generation, live X search, or a second model opinion. This skill explains how to route those tasks through BlockRun with wallet-based payments and spending checks.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to initialize a BlockRun agent wallet with the blockrun-llm SDK.
Explains how to check wallet balance, spending, and wallet address.
Provides examples for paid chat calls to OpenAI, xAI, DeepSeek, and Google models.
Documents xAI live search options for X, web, and news sources.
Includes image generation examples using the BlockRun ImageClient.
Gives basic budget tracking guidance before making paid calls.
limitations: Requires installing and trusting the external blockrun-llm package.
Can spend wallet funds when model calls are executed.
Sends prompts or source data to external AI providers.
Pricing and model availability may change outside the skill file.

use_cases.title: Generate Visual Assets
use_cases.description: Create images through a paid image model when the host assistant cannot generate images directly.

use_cases.title: Check Live X Activity
use_cases.description: Use Grok live search to inspect recent X posts, trends, or account activity with citation support.

use_cases.title: Get a Second Model Opinion
use_cases.description: Route selected prompts to another model for review, comparison, or cost-optimized processing.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Wallet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use BlockRun to show my wallet balance and address. Do not make any paid model calls.

prompt_templates.title: Generate One Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use BlockRun to generate one image of [describe image]. Keep the cost under [budget] and report the final spend.

prompt_templates.title: Search X With Citations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use BlockRun with Grok live search to summarize recent X posts about [topic]. Limit results to [number] sources and include citations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use BlockRun to ask [model name] for a second opinion on [task]. Compare its answer with your own and note cost, strengths, and disagreements.

output_examples.input: Use BlockRun to check my balance.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the wallet balance, wallet address, and whether enough funds are available. It does not run a paid model call.

output_examples.input: Use Grok live search to summarize posts about a product launch.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a concise summary with source citations, search scope, number of sources used, and estimated or final spend.

output_examples.input: Generate a logo concept with DALL-E through BlockRun.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates an image request, returns the generated image link or result details, and reports the cost charged to the wallet.

best_practices: Get explicit user confirmation before any paid BlockRun call.
Set a clear budget and report spending after each task.
Avoid sending secrets, private code, or sensitive data to external models.
anti_patterns: Do not install or upgrade the SDK without user approval.
Do not make paid calls for tasks the local assistant can complete directly.
Do not run live search with broad source counts when a smaller limit is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It documents how to use BlockRun to call paid external AI models from an agent workflow.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: The skill says BlockRun uses a wallet and x402 micropayments instead of direct provider API keys.

faq.question: Can it spend money?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples can spend wallet funds when model calls, live search, or image generation run.

faq.question: Does it send data outside the workspace?
faq.answer: Yes. Prompts and request content may be sent to external providers through BlockRun.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: It combines third-party package installation, hidden wallet storage, paid calls, and external network routing.

faq.question: Is malicious intent confirmed?
faq.answer: No. I found no confirmed malware or prompt injection, but the behavior needs human review before publication.
